lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I am Kandur Hosidius and I am the lord of Hosidius, the city of farmers and chefs. I live here with my wife, Elena, and my two sons, Artur and Pandur.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: My family and I have a peaceful existence here. That's mainly because I care not for the politics of the other houses. I instead devote my time to more meaningful pursuits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Such as?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Ensuring that Kourend is well fed. We may live in gentle times now but who knows how long it will be that way. Sooner or later another war or long winter will hit us. History has proven that.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Hosidius.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hosidius is home to the farmers and chefs of Kourend. We are the main food supplier to the kingdom and we ensure that her people are fed.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: We also own more land than the other Kourend houses. We need the extra space for all of our crops.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do the other houses not get annoyed at how much land you have?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Sometimes. They know it is necessary though. There was once a time when the land was shared equally. But then the great famine came, and Kourend did not have the food to last through it.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: After that, more space was given to Hosidius. We've had the most land ever since.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about your plans.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Plans? My only plan is to keep providing food to this kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what of the Royal Accord of Twill? You must have kept it for a reason.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I did. The Accord has the potential to be a powerful weapon, for words cut deeper than a blade ever can. However, I have no need to use this weapon, not unless the other houses give me reason to.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Until next time.

sections.title: If the player lost the memoir page before using it on the memoir

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I assume you're after that reward I promised you. Here you go.
